items.name_en: Premium Brown OHANELрҹҡ©рҹҡ© Suede double pocket chain bag! This brown double pocket bag is really specialрҹӨ©, the ginger yellow overall body has a great vibe, and it looks very cool when worn! Definitely the king of trendy bright colors. The calfskin material feels super soft and smooth, ready to grab and go, super nice. Chanel Chance really delivers! You can take it away~! I love its simple, casual, and free-spirited style. This color double pocket bag is really beautiful! The material feels like a combination of suede and leather, not as soft as pure suede, but not as hard either, just right. This color is also very good for matching with clothesрҹҳҚрҹҳҚ Dimensions: 34x25x10cm

items.name_en: Premium grade, high version
items.name_cn:
Premium grade, high version
This Dior Toujours vertical bag is stylish and practical, with a unique design. Crafted from black calfskin, it features oversized woven quilting stitching and a spacious interior compartment for various daily essentials. It features a slim leather tie closure for secure storage, along with a CD Lock letter D turn-knob clasp that adjusts on both sides to elevate the style. Medium size, with a top leather handle and a detachable leather shoulder strap, allowing for hand-carry, shoulder carry, or crossbody wear.
Material: Calfskin
Lining: Calfskin
Double opening design: Slim tie and Christian Dior Paris tie with CD Lock clasp
D.I.O.R. charm
Interior card slot
Top leather handle
Detachable leather shoulder strap
Dimensions: 23 x 24 x 16CM
Model: M2836

DiorвқӨпёҸ Toujours Handbag [Black Medium]
This Dior Toujours handbag made its debut at the Fall/Winter 2023 show, featuring a unique, casual, and practical design. Crafted from black calfskin, it features oversized woven quilting stitching. The spacious interior compartment includes a small pocket for neatly organizing daily essentials. It features a CD Lock letter D turn-knob closure and adjustable sides to enhance its elegance. The leather handle has adjustable details for easy hand-carry.
